search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng225 self-harm psychosocial assessment emergency department initial assessment discharge
site:rcem.ac.uk mental health toolkit April 2023 self-harm triage documentation
candidate_sources_rejected: risk scales used to predict suicide or determine discharge
automatic treatment, detention, observation level or safety-plan content
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: NICE advises against using risk tools to predict or determine care.
All actions, legal status, observation and plan content remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children, young people and adults after self-harm; age, method, physical injury, intoxication, neurodevelopmental, safeguarding and legal context require qualified review.
setting_applicability: Emergency physical and psychosocial assessment after self-harm.
UAE_applicability: Assessment principles transfer, but UAE mental-health, consent, capacity, safeguarding, police, observation, liaison and transfer law and pathways govern.
recency_verification: NICE NG225 (2022) and RCEM toolkit (2023) exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Both official sources remain current in their catalogues.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE psychosocial-assessment section supports documenting onset or context, severity or impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, the person’s concerns or goals and clinician risk context without using a prediction score.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ED section supports measured vital data, focused physical and mental assessment, injury severity, appearance, immediate safety and safeguarding only when assessed; it does not predict future suicide.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact safeguarding and discharge section supports recording clinician-decided disposition, clinician-entered plan, completed risk discussion, specialist input and arranged handover only when completed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM triage section independently supports documenting current distress, immediate safety, leaving risk, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status; no future-risk category is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

unresolved_source_gaps: Method, intent, circumstances, injury, current thoughts, access to means, protective factors, capacity, intoxication, psychosocial formulation, legal status, treatment, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
